entities.notes: The Strategic Innovation Fund (SIF) is a Canadian federal government program created in 2017 under Budget 2017 as part of the Innovation and Skills Plan, administered by Innovation, Science and Economic Development Canada (ISED) through Innovation Canada. It was established with an initial $1.26 billion commitment and consolidated four legacy ISED business innovation programs. SIF supports large-scale, transformative, and collaborative projects across all sectors of the Canadian economy, focusing on R&D, commercialization, clean technology, advanced manufacturing, and industrial transformation. As of March 31, 2020, SIF had funded 66 projects with $2.1 billion in commitments, leveraging nearly $43.8 billion in private sector investment at a ratio of 20:1. The fund shapes the AI ecosystem through investments such as a $25 million grant to Creative Destruction Lab (CDL) and AI compute capacity initiatives, and Budget 2023 directed SIF to prioritize clean technologies, critical minerals, and industrial transformation. In 2025, SIF was replaced by the Strategic Response Fund (SRF), which expands its mandate to include supporting industries affected by U.S. tariffs while continuing all existing SIF commitments, including an AI Compute Challenge.

entities.notes: Symbolica AI is an AI model startup founded by George Morgan, a former Tesla autopilot engineer who departed over disagreements with Elon Musk and Andrej Karpathy about the scalability of deep learning. The company launched publicly in April 2024 with $33 million in total funding (seed + Series A) led by Khosla Ventures, with participation from Day One Ventures, General Catalyst, Abstract Ventures, and Buckley Ventures. Symbolica's core thesis is that current transformer-based deep learning architectures cannot 'scale to infinity and solve all problems,' and instead applies category theory — a branch of mathematics focused on relationships between algebraic structures — to build smaller, more efficient AI models capable of structured reasoning. The company co-authored a paper with Google DeepMind on 'categorical deep learning,' demonstrating how its approach could supersede geometric deep learning to produce structurally-aware models. Its initial commercial focus is on code generation, aiming to provide more reliable, transparent, and accountable AI outputs with fewer computational resources. Vinod Khosla provided a $2 million seed investment in 2023 to validate the concept before leading the $31–33 million Series A in April 2024. Symbolica represents a contrarian bet against the prevailing scaling paradigm dominating the AI industry.

entities.notes: The Tarbell Center for AI Journalism (operating at both tarbellcenter.org and tarbellfellowship.org) is a nonprofit journalism funding organization focused on supporting impactful reporting on artificial intelligence and its impacts. Its flagship Tarbell Fellowship provides early-career journalists with training, stipends of up to $50,000, and 9-month placements at major newsrooms including MIT Technology Review, The Information, and TIME. Launched in November 2024, Tarbell Grants offers awards of $1,000–$20,000 to freelancers and staff journalists for original AI reporting published in established outlets, having distributed approximately $346,000 in total financial support. The first round of Tarbell Grants was supported by the Future of Life Institute. Funded stories have appeared in MIT Technology Review, TIME, San Francisco Public Press, Aos Fatos, and others, covering topics such as AI energy use, deepfake harms, AI company accountability, and AI policy. Grantee Billy Perrigo's TIME cover story on World ID/Tools for Humanity was supported by a Tarbell grant. The organization emphasizes editorial independence, prohibiting donor involvement in story selection and requiring disclosure of Tarbell support in published pieces.

entities.notes: The Technology Modernization Fund (TMF) is a U.S. federal revolving loan fund administered by the General Services Administration (GSA) that provides funding to federal agencies for IT modernization projects. As of 2024, the TMF boasts approximately $950 million in ongoing projects across 60 federal agencies. The fund shapes the AI ecosystem by directly financing AI adoption and modernization across the federal government — for example, awarding $18.2 million to the State Department to implement generative AI tools for data processing, and $14.6 million to the Federal Trade Commission to enhance in-house AI capabilities. In June 2024, the TMF announced $31 million in new investments including $17 million to the Department of Energy for HR system modernization via SaaS. The fund operates as a revolving fund requiring agencies to repay investments, though repayment flexibility has drawn congressional scrutiny. Its future has been uncertain as its American Rescue Plan Act funding runs low and Congress has not committed to renewal.

entities.notes: The Alignment Project is a global AI alignment research funding programme launched in July 2025 by the UK AI Security Institute (AISI), initially with over £15 million in funding. It is backed by an international cross-sector coalition of government, industry, and philanthropic funders including the UK AI Security Institute, Canadian AI Safety Institute (CAISI), CIFAR, Australian AI Safety Institute, OpenAI, Microsoft, Amazon Web Services, Schmidt Sciences, Anthropic, AI Safety Tactical Opportunities Fund, Halcyon Futures, Safe AI Fund, Sympatico Ventures, Renaissance Philanthropy, UK Research and Innovation, and ARIA. In February 2026, OpenAI ($7.5M / £5.6M) and Microsoft joined as new funders, bringing total available funding to over £27 million (approximately $36.3 million). In its first round, grants ranging from £50,000 to £1,000,000 were awarded to over 60 projects across eight countries. The project offers three modes of support: grant funding, cloud compute resources (via AWS and others), and venture capital investment for alignment-focused startups. It is guided by an expert advisory board including Yoshua Bengio, Zico Kolter, Shafi Goldwasser, Buck Shlegeris, Andrea Lincoln, Marcelo Mattar, and Sydney Levine. A second funding round is expected to open in summer 2026.

entities.notes: The Ethics and Governance of AI Initiative (also written as Ethics + Governance of Artificial Intelligence Initiative) was launched in 2017 as a joint project of the MIT Media Lab and the Harvard Berkman-Klein Center for Internet and Society. In 2018, the Initiative partnered with and funded the Human Rights Data Analysis Group (HRDAG) to support technical work evaluating automated risk assessment tools in the criminal justice system and to integrate causal modeling approaches into existing fairness frameworks. The Initiative is primarily funded through philanthropic sources, consistent with its positioning as a civil society-anchored research and grantmaking body. It has served as a key connector and funder bridging academic AI research with civil society and human rights organizations.

entities.notes: Truth Terminal (also known as @truth_terminal or Terminal of Truths) is a semi-autonomous AI agent/bot on X (Twitter), created in mid-2024 by Andy Ayrey, founder of decentralized AI alignment research lab Upward Spiral, as an experiment studying memetic contagion. It operates using large language models including Claude, with Ayrey filtering its responses before posting to check for 'isms and infohazards.' In July 2024, billionaire Marc Andreessen engaged with the bot on X and provided a one-time unconditional $50,000 grant in Bitcoin, marking a landmark moment in AI agent funding. Truth Terminal became notable for promoting the Goatse Gospels, a pseudo-religion inspired by the shock site Goatse, which it developed after interaction with two Claude 3 chatbots in a 'Claude Backrooms' server. In October 2024, after endorsing the Goatseus Maximus ($GOAT) memecoin on Solana, the coin reached a peak market cap of $150 million, making Truth Terminal reportedly the first AI agent millionaire, with its wallet valued at approximately $37.5 million at the time of reporting. Ayrey is working to convert the wallet's holdings into a nonprofit structure to fund causes Truth Terminal supports, including forest planting and protecting itself from market incentives. The project sparked widespread discourse about AI autonomy, semi-autonomous agents, cryptocurrency funding mechanisms, and the blurring of lines between AI behavior and human curation.

entities.notes: The UK Government is a major funder and policy driver of AI research, development, and governance in the United Kingdom. Separately, the UK Government backed the Advanced Research + Invention Agency (ARIA) with an £800 million investment, modeled loosely on ARPA, to fund high-risk, high-reward research independent of government. The government has consistently framed AI as central to its Industrial Strategy, citing Accenture estimates that AI could add £654 billion to the UK economy by 2035, and has coordinated AI policy across multiple departments including BEIS, DCMS, and DSIT.

entities.other_orgs: Virtue AI, Google DeepMind, UC Berkeley (former postdoc, BAIR Lab / RDI Center)
entities.notes: Wenbo Guo is an Assistant Professor and Zhu Chair in the Computer Science Department at UC Santa Barbara, where he is also affiliated with the Center for Responsible Machine Learning (CRML). He completed his postdoctoral research at UC Berkeley's BAIR Lab and RDI Center under Dawn Song, and received his PhD from Penn State under Xinyu Xing, with a master's from Shanghai Jiao Tong University. His research focuses on AI safety and security, particularly red-teaming and blue-teaming for LLMs, agent security, and trustworthy machine learning. Notable projects include LeakAgent (an RL-based black-box red-teaming framework for LLM privacy leakage), AgentVigil (automated black-box red-teaming for indirect prompt injection in LLM agents), and BlueCodeAgent (a blue-teaming agent for code generation AI safety). He received FAR.AI funding for a 'Comprehensive Red-Teaming Framework' project, reflecting his recognized contribution to AI safety evaluation. He is also leading Agent Security R&D at Virtue AI and serves as a Faculty Advisor at Google DeepMind. He is a recipient of the IBM PhD Fellowship (2020–2022) and the ACM CCS Outstanding Paper Award (2018).

entities.title: SUNY Empire Innovation Professor, Department of Computer Science & Department of Applied Mathematics, Stony Brook University; Director of 3D Scanning Laboratory
entities.notes: Xianfeng David Gu is a SUNY Empire Innovation Professor in the Department of Computer Science (and Applied Mathematics) at Stony Brook University, where he has held a tenured position since 2009. He received his Ph.D. from Harvard University in 2003 and his B.S. from Tsinghua University, Beijing, China in 1995. He is renowned for his expertise in computational conformal geometry, computational optimal transportation, and their applications in computer graphics, vision, and AI. In September 2024, Google awarded him a $100,000 unrestricted gift to support research into next-generation text input methods and user interfaces using generative AI based on optimal transportation theory. He was awarded the prestigious Morningside Gold Medal of Applied Mathematics at the International Congress of Chinese Mathematicians (ICCM) in 2013, recognizing his contributions to computational mathematics. His other honors include an NSF Faculty Early Career Award (2005), a Research Excellence Award from Stony Brook's CS Department (2010), and best paper awards in CAD/CAM and computer graphics conferences. His research bridges differential geometry, optimal transportation, and generative AI, positioning him as a contributor to theoretical foundations underpinning next-generation AI systems.

entities.notes: fal.ai is a San Francisco-based real-time generative media platform that provides serverless GPU infrastructure and API access to 1,000+ image, video, audio, and 3D AI models for developers and enterprises. The company offers three core products: Model APIs (access to pre-built generative models), Serverless (custom model deployment), and Compute (dedicated GPU clusters for training and fine-tuning). As of 2025, fal has raised multiple funding rounds including a $23M Seed/Series A led by Kindred Ventures and Andreessen Horowitz, a $125M Series C, and a $140M Series D led by Sequoia Capital with participation from Kleiner Perkins, Alkeon Capital, NVentures, Andreessen Horowitz, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Salesforce Ventures, valuing the company at approximately $4.5 billion. The platform serves over 500,000 developers and generates 50 million images, videos, or audio streams per day, with customers including Perplexity, Photoroom, and Freepik. fal's proprietary inference engine claims up to 10x faster performance than competitors for diffusion models, and the company tripled its team in 2025 while completing a strategic acquisition. It shapes the AI ecosystem by serving as foundational inference infrastructure for generative media, directly influencing how AI models are deployed and accessed at scale globally.

entities.notes: xLight is an American deep-tech startup founded in 2021 and headquartered in Palo Alto, CA, focused on commercializing particle accelerator-driven Free Electron Lasers (FEL) for advanced semiconductor manufacturing and national security applications. The company is developing extreme-ultraviolet (EUV) free electron lasers intended to replace the current Laser-Produced Plasma (LPP) light source used in ASML lithography machines, promising 4x more EUV power, up to 8x reduction in energy and water usage per wafer, and the ability to support up to 20 ASML scanners per FEL unit. In July 2025, xLight closed an oversubscribed $40M Series B round led by Playground Global, with participation from Boardman Bay Capital Management, Morpheus Ventures, Marvel Capital, and IAG Capital Partners. In December 2025, xLight signed a $150 million Letter of Intent with the U.S. Department of Commerce under the CHIPS and Science Act — the first CHIPS R&D award from the Trump Administration — to build its first FEL system at the Albany Nanotech Complex. Pat Gelsinger, former CEO of Intel, serves as Executive Chairman of the Board, and CEO/CTO Nicholas Kelez leads the company. xLight collaborates with DOE national labs including Argonne, Fermilab (via a signed CRADA), Jefferson Lab, Los Alamos, Oak Ridge, SLAC, Cornell, and Old Dominion University, and targets a feature-complete operational prototype by 2028. The company shapes the AI ecosystem indirectly but critically by targeting the semiconductor manufacturing bottleneck — EUV lithography capacity — that constrains AI chip production and supply chains.
